macro_segments.title: Yosef's Weeping and Symbolic Meaning: Necks, Temples, and Future Destruction

micro_segments.title: Yosef fell on Binyamin's neck and wept: Why plural 'necks'?
Rabbi Elazar: Yosef wept for two Temples that would be destroyed in Binyamin's portion
Binyamin wept for the destruction of Mishkan Shiloh in Yosef's portion
Yosef's reassurance: 'Behold, your eyes see that I speak to you' - No grudge against brothers
Ten donkeys carrying Egypt's bounty; old wine for elderly appreciation

macro_segments.title: Brothers' Prostration and Power Dynamics: Fox Metaphor and Yosef's Leadership

micro_segments.title: After Yaakov's death, brothers prostrate themselves before Yosef fearing retaliation
Midrash: 'A fox in his time you must bow to him' - explaining power dynamics
Proper context: Yaakov bowing on his bed - Yosef's temporal power justified
Yosef consoles brothers with spiritual metaphor: Ten candles cannot extinguish one candle

macro_segments.title: Return to Megillah: Light, Joy, Rejoicing, and Honor as Four Themes of Purim

micro_segments.title: Layehudim hayeta orah vesimkhah vesason vikar - Light, joy, rejoicing, honor
Rav Yehudah: Orah is Torah; ner mitzvah v'Torah or
Simkhah is Yom Tov; Sason is milah; Vikar is tefillin
Rashi: Haman decreed against both life and religious practice (Torah, holidays, circumcision, tefillin)

macro_segments.title: Reading the Ten Sons of Haman: Breath, Gallows, and Scribal Traditions

micro_segments.title: Rav Adda: Aseret benei Haman must be said in one breath - all died simultaneously
Tosafot: One breath is l'chatchilah; b'diavad yatza without it
Vav of Vaizata stretched like a river pole - symbolizes the gallows
Rabbi Chanina Bar Pappa: Brick-on-brick pattern for songs; aseret benei Haman in straight columns
All songs in Tanach staggered; only aseret benei Haman and melachei Kena'an in straight rows
Reason: shelo tehe tekuma lemapaltan - no rising to their fall; complete collapse symbolized

macro_segments.title: The Dramatic Turn in Achashverosh's Response to Esther

micro_segments.title: Vayomer hamelech - shift from fury to offer in middle of verse
Rabbi Abbahu: Angel came and slapped Achashverosh's mouth, changing his speech
Uvevo'ah lifnei hamelech - amar im hasefer - read it from the scroll

macro_segments.title: Divrei Shalom Ve'emet and Scribal Requirements for the Scroll

micro_segments.title: Divrei shalom ve'emet - requires sirtut like the truth of Torah
Rashi vs. Tosafot on sirtut requirement for sefer Torah and mezuzah

macro_segments.title: Acceptance of Purim Days and Connection to Fasting and Esther's Ordinance

micro_segments.title: Um'amar Esther kiyam - Esther's ordinance established these Purim days
Divrei ha-tzomot ve-za'akatam - fasting and crying connected to fulfillment
Interpretation: Through fasting and Esther's words, Purim days were validated and preserved

macro_segments.title: Mordechai's Status: Second to the King and Descent in Rank

micro_segments.title: Ki Mordechai ha-Yehudi mishneh la-melech - not to all his brothers but to most
Rav Yosef: Talmud Torah greater than saving lives - Mordechai suffered for leaving study
Mordechai's rank: Fifth in Ezra's list originally; Sixth after Purim story

macro_segments.title: Talmud Torah Greater Than All: Comparative Statements on Learning's Primacy

micro_segments.title: Rava: Talmud Torah greater than building the Beit HaMikdash
Ezra stayed in Bavel until his rebbe died - couldn't leave study for rebuilding
Tension between halakha (suspend Torah for other mitzvot) and aggada (Torah supreme)
Rava bar bar Rav Yitzchak: Talmud Torah greater than honoring parents
Yaakov's fourteen years hidden in Beit Ever - not punished for learning instead of serving father
Age calculation: Yaakov 137 when he descended; missing 14 years explained as study time
Yosef's 22-year absence parallels Yaakov's 22-year gap; suggests justice in fate

macro_segments.title: Technical Requirements for Reading the Megillah: Language, Scroll, Order, and Intent

micro_segments.title: Hakorei es HaMegilla lemafrea lo yatza - cannot read backwards
Kara al peh, Targum, bechol lashon - cannot read from memory, translation, or other languages
Exception: Foreigner hearing Hebrew is yotzei; Hebrew-speaker hearing translation is not
Karah seirugin, umisnamnem - reading with breaks or dozing off is permissible
Writing, teaching, or correcting - requires conscious intent (kavana) to fulfill mitzvah
Ketuba b'sam, sikra, kumus - must be written with proper ink on klaf, not paper
Ktiv ashurit al hasefer vidyo - written in Hebrew letters, on parchment, with ink

macro_segments.title: Source and Logic for Backward Reading Prohibition: Time and Writing Order

micro_segments.title: Rabba: Verse kkisavam u'chezmanim - as written and in their times
Logic: Time doesn't go backward; therefore writing cannot be read backward
Gemara clarifies: 'Asiya' in the verse, not 'Keria' - about observance, not reading
Correction: From 'nizkarim ve-naasim' - remembrance connected to action, same order applies
Applied to Hallel, Shema, and Tefilla - all must be in proper order per various verses

macro_segments.title: Shema: Debate Between Rabbi and Rabbanan on Language and Verbalization

micro_segments.title: Beraitah: Rabbi requires Shema in Hebrew; Rabbanan allow any language
Rabbi's source: Verse 'v'hayu v'hayu' - exact words as written
Rabbanan's source: 'Shema Yisrael' - listen however you understand
Rabbi: Must hear words spoken aloud; Rabbanan: Hearing not necessary, understanding suffices
Conceptual link: Rabbi focuses on ritual form; Rabbanan on meaning and understanding
Gemara's resolution: Neither position requires both conditions; verses specify one aspect each

macro_segments.title: Tefilla: Shemoneh Esrei Arranged in Order by Shimon Hapakuli

micro_segments.title: Beraitah: Shimon Hapakuli arranged 18 blessings before Rabban Gamliel in Yavneh
Alternative tradition: 120 elders including prophets arranged the 18 blessings
Opening praises: Avot (fathers), Gevurot (strength), Kedushot (holiness) from verses
Avot allows finite human to approach infinite God; Gevurot and Kedushot recognize gap
